name: Ascent to Montiel viewpoint - Circular from Paterna
name_original: Subida al mirador de Montiel — Circular desde Paterna
region_intro: Road cycling around Paterna offers diverse terrain, from mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The region benefits from its close connection to Valencia's extensive cycling infrastructure, including the Turia River Park, which provides a pleasant green corridor for cycling. Cyclists can explore picturesque villages and quiet secondary roads, with options ranging from moderate rides to longer, more demanding paths that incorporate nearby natural areas like Sierra Calderona Natural Park. This variety ensures routes cater to different fitness levels and preferences. Best road cycling routes around Paterna The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to Montiel viewpoint - Circular from Paterna, a 46.5 miles (74.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route features a notable ascent to a viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Oronet Pass – Oronet Pass loop from La Canyada, a moderate 35.1 miles (56.5 km) path. This route navigates through the Oronet Pass, known for its quiet roads and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Path to Barranco de Portaceli – Pla de Llit Recreation Area loop from Paterna, a 30.5 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through the Barranco de Portaceli and towards the Pla de Llit Recreation Area, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Paterna is defined by its proximity to the Turia River, varied terrain including hills and quiet secondary roads, and access to natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Paterna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 35 reviews. More than 2,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Paterna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Paterna?
A: The region around Paterna offers diverse terrain for road cycling. You'll find routes with mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels, as well as more challenging paths with significant elevation gain. Some routes may include unpaved segments. The area benefits from its connection to Valencia's extensive cycling infrastructure, including the Turia River Park, which provides a pleasant green corridor. You can also explore picturesque villages and quiet secondary roads, with options ranging from moderate rides to more demanding ascents into natural areas like Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Paterna area?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Paterna available on komoot, catering to various fitness levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Paterna?
A: Yes, Paterna offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature mostly paved surfaces and less significant elevation changes. You can find 5 easy routes in the area, providing a gentle introduction to road cycling in the region.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders near Paterna?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Paterna offers routes with significant elevation gain and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20620775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antenas de Pedralba – El Gabaldón Pass loop from la Vallesa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 91 kilometers with more than 1000 meters of ascent. The region also features routes through mountain passes like Oronet, known for their beautiful views and quiet roads.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Paterna?
A: Road cycling routes around Paterna offer access to a variety of natural features and landmarks. You can explore areas near the Turia River, which is transformed into the Turia Gardens, a significant recreational space. Routes may also lead you past highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1085458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rodana Gran&lt;/a&gt; summit, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2674584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carasoles Geodetic Point&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4059492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Montiel&lt;/a&gt;. The region's outstanding natural setting includes diverse landscapes, from wetlands to mountains.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Paterna?
A: The road cycling routes in Paterna are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 35 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the proximity to the Turia River, and the access to natural parks. Over 2,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, highlighting its appeal.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Paterna?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Paterna are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858830555/ascent-to-montiel-viewpoint-circular-from-paterna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Montiel viewpoint - Circular from Paterna&lt;/a&gt; is a 74.8 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oronet Pass – Oronet Pass loop from La Canyada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 56.5 km path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Paterna?
A: The region around Paterna, being close to Valencia, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some mountain passes might be cooler.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Paterna using public transport?
A: Paterna benefits from its close connection to Valencia's infrastructure, which includes a bike-friendly environment and cycle paths. While specific public transport options for carrying bikes directly to trailheads might vary, the general accessibility of Paterna from Valencia via routes that often incorporate the Turia River Park suggests good connectivity. For detailed public transport information, it's best to check local transport services.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes in the Paterna area offer scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858830555/ascent-to-montiel-viewpoint-circular-from-paterna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Montiel viewpoint - Circular from Paterna&lt;/a&gt;, as its name suggests, leads to a notable viewpoint with panoramic vistas. The Oronet Pass is also known for its beautiful views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2674584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carasoles Geodetic Point&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that offers great perspectives of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling near Paterna?
A: While road cycling routes can vary in difficulty, the proximity to the Turia River Park provides a pleasant and extended path that is generally flat and suitable for families. For specific family-friendly road cycling routes, look for the 'easy' graded routes on komoot, which typically have less elevation and are shorter in distance, making them more accessible for a wider range of ages and abilities.

Q: What kind of cultural or historical attractions can I find near the cycling routes in Paterna?
A: Paterna itself offers unique historical and cultural attractions. You can explore the unique Cave Houses (Cuevas del Batán) carved into rock faces, or visit The Tower, an Arabic watchtower offering panoramic views. The El Calvario area features the city's clock tower and a panoramic terrace. The easy cycling connection to Valencia also allows exploration of iconic city attractions like the City of Arts and Sciences and Valencia Cathedral.
tips:
The Sanctuary offers amazing views from the highest point to the entire region.
For those who are interested, the Sanctuary can also be toured and visited inside.
Undoubtedly an ideal place to catch some air and appreciate some very nice views.
